function_explanation: Performs a 1D Fast Fourier Transform on a specified dimension of a multi-dimensional tensor using orthonormal normalization.
function_docstring:
"""Performs a 1-dimensional Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) on a specified dimension of a multi-dimensional tensor.

    The function applies the FFT shift to center the zero-frequency component in the middle of the spectrum, performs a 1D FFT over the specified dimension with orthonormal normalization, and then shifts the zero-frequency component back to its original location.

    Args:
        arr (torch.Tensor): A multi-dimensional tensor on which the 1D FFT is applied.
        n (int): The dimension along which to perform the 1D FFT.

    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: The transformed tensor of the same shape as `arr` with the FFT applied along the specified dimension.
    """
usage_example:
# Example usage of fft1d
import torch

# Create a sample 3D tensor
sample_tensor = torch.rand(8, 16, 32)  # example shape, can be any dimensions

# Perform 1D FFT along the second dimension (index 1)
transformed_tensor = fft1d(sample_tensor, n=1)
print(transformed_tensor)
